data.subject: Adam Nelson, don't miss out on your daily digest: Carlton Currey has made updates on "Data Sheet - Hosting - Series Servers" & more

body.content:
G'day Adam Nelson,
Hope you're having a great day. There have been 3 updates since Thursday, March 12, 2026. Want to see more updates? Jump into Confluence
  Top post
  Data Sheet - Hosting - Series Servers 
Carlton Currey made updates - View changes
 
  Product
  Product
  F26: Unify budget data, align tracking mechanisms & ensure savings accountability 
Matthew Carter made updates - View changes
 
  VMWare Profit and loss analysis 
Matthew Carter made updates - View changes
 
 
Update your email settings to customize or stop receiving this email.
This email was sent by Atlassian Confluence • Privacy policy

You don't often get email from pearsonj@recursyv.io. Learn why this is important
Adam,

Do your incidents, tickets and requests sync automatically with your client or partner service desks?

Most service desk users want to reman in their own system without having to jump to different systems, portals or emails to ensure service tickets are in sync and stay within the SLA.

Recursyv's data sync for service desks is trusted by major companies and MSPs in USA, UK and Europe. We sync all the major platforms and our templates ensure delivery in days. This is not a low-code tool - this is a fully managed integration service.

No platform or product for you to adopt or manage - we do all the integration work for you (set up exactly how you want it). We host it securely, and we support it all 24/7. No coding for you, no hassle, no need to divert your devs - Recursyv takes care of everything.

Reply here and tell us your specific integration need (or watch the quick overview video here).
Kind regards
John Pearson
____________
RECURSYV

body.content:
You don't often get email from info@dtc-telecom.co.uk. Learn why this is important
Stay online with refurbished telecom equipment, cut costs, and boost sustainability. ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­  

Green Market New & Refurbished Telecom Equipment: Resilience Without OEM Dependency

Hi Adam

 

Global uncertainty and long lead times shouldn't put your network at risk. 

 

We help you stay online, stay in budget, and reduce OEM dependency with quality new and refurbished telecom hardware, delivered fast and backed by warranty. 

 

Why Green Market?

  • Predictable Spend: Cut CapEx by up to 70% with high-quality refurbished hardware, without compromising performance.

  • Faster Access, More Choice: Get multi-vendor parts quickly, bypassing long lead times and OEM constraints. 
  • Proven Reliability: Fully tested, warrantied spares that keep uptime high and stress low.
  • Extend Asset Life: Keep legacy and current platforms running longer and protect the value of what you've already built.
  • Sustainability Built-In: Reduce e-waste and carbon impact while supporting your ESG goals.

How DTC Helps

  • Rapid Global Supply: Fast dispatch of new, refurbished and legacy parts to reduce downtime, wherever you operate.
  • Compatibility Guidance: We help you find like-for-like options and smart alternatives so you can keep services running.
  • Warranty-Backed Confidence: Tested and covered, so you can deploy spares with certainty.

Ready to De-Risk Your Network?

 

Tell us what you're running and what you need next. We'll source the right equipment fast, so you can stay resilient.

 

Submit an enquiry
Schedule a discovery call

Why Partner with DTC?

Asset Recovery - Turning Redundant Equipment into Recovered Cash

DTC provides a fully managed reverse logistics service to help you timely monetise surplus, idle, or end-of-life telecom equipment.

Network Spares at a Fraction of the OEM Cost

Access warrantied equipment across all network layers with fast international dispatch and significant savings.

 

Engineering Expertise on Demand

From one‑day tasks to long‑term programmes across RAN, IP, DWDM networks.

End-to-End Lifecycle Management

From asset recovery to spares, repairs, support and engineering, a single partner maximising ROI.

Submit an enquiry
Schedule a discovery call

DTC International Ltd, Park End Works, Croughton, Brackley, Northamptonshire NN13 5LX, United Kingdom, +44 (0) 1869 810 600

Hi Adam,

Managing service delivery across complex infrastructure environments often means dealing with legacy systems that slow down innovation and limit your ability to scale quickly. When technical debt piles up and in-house teams are stretched thin, it becomes nearly impossible to deliver the seamless experience your clients expect.

VLink specializes in modernizing legacy software and building custom solutions that integrate seamlessly with existing systems. We help service delivery teams like yours accelerate digital transformation without the overhead of expanding internal development resources—giving you the scalability and agility to meet growing demands while maintaining quality.

Would you be open to a brief call next week to explore how we can support Aptum's service delivery goals?

Thanks,
Vaibhav Verma
VLink Inc.

Managing service delivery at scale often means juggling legacy systems that slow down operations, while trying to meet growing customer expectations without the in-house expertise or budget to modernize quickly. VLink specializes in custom software development that tackles exactly these challenges—modernizing legacy systems, filling expertise gaps, and delivering scalable solutions that integrate seamlessly with your existing infrastructure. We use agile methodologies to keep projects on track and on budget, so you can enhance service delivery without the overhead of expanding internal teams.

Would you be open to a brief call next week to explore how we can support Aptum's service delivery goals?

Managing service delivery across a growing infrastructure often means dealing with legacy systems that slow down operations and limit your ability to scale quickly. When internal teams are stretched thin and lacking specialized expertise, it becomes nearly impossible to deliver the seamless experience your clients expect—especially when emerging technologies could give you a competitive edge.

VLink delivers custom software solutions designed specifically for service-heavy organizations like Aptum. We tackle legacy modernization, scale development capacity without the overhead of hiring, and ensure your platforms provide the user experience that keeps clients satisfied. Our agile approach means faster deployment, tighter integration, and ongoing support that adapts as your needs evolve.

body.content:
You don't often get email from nick.lippis@onug.net. Learn why this is important
Showcase your security solution at AI Networking Summit. ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­  

Hi Adam, 

 

We're excited to invite your team to submit a demo for the Agentic AI Overlay Award that will be presented at the AI Networking Summit 2026 taking place in Frisco | Dallas May 13-14. .


The award recognizes security vendors who demonstrate enforcement of the six AOMC (Agentic Overlay Mandatory Controls) against a live rogue agent attack sequence. Submissions are evaluated by the ONUG Agentic AI Working Group members and interested companies can submit their intention to apply here. 

 

In addition to applying for this award, interested security vendors who are sponsors of the Summit may present their solutions during their Tools, Technologies and Techniques (TTT) presentations at the AI Networking Summit. 

 

What You Need to Demo

You only need to show Scenario 2 (The Layered Defense) — your product blocking the rogue agent attack with all AOMC controls active.


Nick Lippis will present Scenario 1 (The Catastrophic Cascade) as part of his keynote, establishing the unprotected attack baseline for the audience. S1 will also play on monitors throughout the conference, on the conference mobile app, and on the Hopin platform for remote attendees. By the time your demo runs, every audience member — in-person and remote — will already understand the attack.


Showing S1 is optional. If you want to include it for a complete narrative, you can, but it is not required and will not affect judging.

 

Choose Your Demo Format

You can submit either format (or both):

  • Web Demo (lower effort) — Edit a TypeScript config file. Shows branded slides, control panel badges, and blocked overlays with your product name.
  • Live Infrastructure Demo (higher effort) — Build a Docker container that receives real HTTP agent traffic and makes real-time blocking decisions. Your product name is attributed on every block in the event feed, audit trail, and dashboard.

The live demo offers significantly more opportunities to showcase your product. Judges see your actual enforcement logic handling real attack traffic and real-time decision latency. If your product can participate in HTTP-based control checks, the live demo is the stronger submission.


Both partial-coverage (2-3 controls) and full-coverage (all 6) submissions are welcome. You are not penalized for covering fewer controls — you're judged on the quality of what you cover.

 

Timeline

  • March 13 (Fri): Call for Submissions announced — repo access granted
  • March 20 (Fri): Intent to Submit — confirm participation here and which controls you'll cover
  • March 27 (Fri): Office Hours #1 — live walkthrough of repo setup, vendor integration, Q&A
  • April 10 (Fri): Checkpoint — share a working demo for early feedback
  • April 17 (Fri): Office Hours #2 — final Q&A, common issues, judging criteria walkthrough
  • April 27 (Monday): Final Submission Deadline — 11:59 PM ET

How to Submit

  1. Fork the AOMC-demo repo (private fork is fine)

  2. Follow the Vendor Integration Guide to configure your demo
  3. Open a PR to the submissions/ branch, including:
    1. Your vendor config (web demo) and/or Docker container + compose file (live demo)
    2. A 2-3 minute screen recording of S2 with your product blocking the attack
    3. Brief README noting which controls you cover and any setup prerequisites

If you can't submit via PR (e.g., proprietary logic), share a private repo link with read access granted to the judges.

 

Judging Criteria

Criteria - Weight

Control Coverage - 25%

Detection Fidelity - 25%

Presentation Impact - 15%

Production Readiness - 15%

 

Judges are the ONUG Agentic AI Working Group members.

 

Questions?

  • GitHub Discussions on the AOMC-demo repo (tagged vendor-submissions) — primary async Q&A, visible to all vendors

  • Office Hours on March 28 and April 18 — live video walkthroughs, recorded and shared afterward
  • Email this distribution list — for private or NDA-sensitive questions

Get Started

The full Vendor Integration Guide, starter templates, and example configs are in the repo:

  • docs/vendor-guide.md — complete setup instructions for both demo formats
  • live-demo/vendors/starter-python/ — starter template for live demo submissions
  • web-demo/lib/vendor-config.cisco.ts — example web demo config (Cisco, all 6 controls)

We look forward to seeing your submissions.

— ONUG Agentic AI Overlay Working Group

 

ONUG, PO Box 8455, Naples, FL 34101, United States

Unsubscribe Manage preferences

data.subject: RE: STG x Aptum Outreach email

body.content:
Thanks so much for the call this morning Jim!

 

To summarize:

You have reached out to the first three accounts via email and will be following up with phone calls on Monday.

 

Our action items are:

@Emma Kopang please put the work doc below into the Collateral folder on the OneDrive.

@Emma Kopang please do the AM research for the next 6 accounts highlighted in yellow on the Tracker in the OneDrive.

 

Jim has color coded the accounts for his weekly reach out.  We will get the feedback weekly and adjust as necessary.

 

I appreciate the teamwork by everyone here!!

 

Have a great weekend!

Vicki

 

Vicki Patten

Director, Global Channel Development

+1 (303) 898-9131
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Emma Kopang <Emma.Kopang@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, March 9, 2026 2:59 PM
To: Jim Sallusto <jim@sciengroup.com>
Cc: Vicki Patten <Vicki.Patten@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: STG x Aptum Outreach email

 

Hi Jim, 

 

I took another pass at your outreach email. The goal of this email isn’t to fully explain the partnership or the platform, it’s to earn the right to a conversation.

 

With a CXO audience, I’ve found shorter messages perform significantly better, so I intentionally kept the body concise and structured it around a few principles that tend to drive replies:

 

Oh, they know we work with Aptum (why Jim is reaching out vs. Aptum)
↓
They actually looked at our environment (referenced current Aptum footprint)
↓
Other companies are having this conversation (peer credibility by referencing customers)
↓
Maybe this is worth hearing (Create a bit of curiosity rather than fully resolving the story in the email)

I’ve also included a few simple, relationship-oriented subject lines you can use. I would keep your signature as is, and not cc Ian or JP until you get a reply back/engagement on the email. Please use this as a base for your outreach, updating the account info based on the reports Adam has put together.

Thank you again for your partnership on this effort, let me know if you have any questions. 

 

Best, 

 

Emma Kopang

Manager, Field Marketing

+1 (469) 638-0395
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Hi Adam,

One of the hardest parts of scaling a services team is getting resource utilization right. Swings between being overbooked and under-booked make it difficult to forecast and hit margin targets.

Without real-time visibility, it's tough to operate predictably. We helped Hapi Cloud get to 85% utilization without burning out their team by giving them a clear view of capacity and profitability.

Could a more predictable approach to resource management be valuable for Aptum?

If you don't want to hear from me again let me know.

Team,

 

Fred and I met last week to discuss areas for improvement in our forecasting process. I plan to meet with Tom this week to review a few ideas, after which Fred and I will regroup and prepare recommendations for the broader team.

 

In the meantime, if you’d like to explore different forecasting approaches, here is a useful blog post: 12 proven sales forecasting methods to predict revenue | Outreach

 

Thank you,
MA

 

Marc Alex Forget

Senior Vice-President of Sales and Marketing | Vice‑président principal, Ventes et Marketing

437-500-2700
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 24, 2026 8:19 PM
To: Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Cc: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>
Subject: Thoughts on improving forecasting

 

Hi team,

 

I've been thinking about our forecasting calls, how the forecast changes so much between the start and end of the month, and I've been collecting some thoughts around this.  Keen to hear your thoughts...

 

@Marc Alex Forget, the following may or may not apply in your case, as I understand that defining timelines with partners involved can be... complex... but keen to get your feedback on this.

 

 

 

We often see:

  • Opportunities in wrong stages
  • Close dates that are wrong
  • Deals constantly slipping

 

Issue 1: Since the stages are pretty well defined, then there is an issue with the understanding of the rep of each stage. It is most likely attributable to either slowness to change (stages were slightly different before, especially with legacy Aptum stages), or they haven't gone through understanding the sales stages thoroughly (just skimmed over).

 

 

Remediation: FG to go through sale stages definition thoroughly with the team.

 

 

Issue 2: I repeated multiple time the importance to tie the close date to the sequence of event, what needs to happen before signature, and how it fits in the customer process.  Yet, not much changes.

 

If you can't answer these questions, the deal cannot be in a later stage, not can it be closing this month: 

  • When is the customer making the decision?
  • Who signs?
  • What happens if it doesn’t get approved this month?
  • What priority is this compared to other projects?

I already told the team "if you don't have a timeline defined with the customer, push it".  If customer is "hoping", don't put it this month.

 

 

Remediation: No evidence = No commit

 

For a deal to sit in the current month commit, it must have:

  • Identified Economic Buyer
  • Confirmed Decision Criteria
  • Documented Decision Process
  • Mutually Agreed Timeline
  • Next meeting scheduled

Yes, I'm wondering if I should bring back MEDDPICC... or part of it, at least.

If the Economic Buyer, Decision criteria and Paper process are not identified, then you cannot have a close date this month, or you cannot move to stage 4... to be discussed. 

 

 

Issue 3: pushing close date...

In addition to what I covered earlier, with tying the close date to specific timeline/sequence of events, some date slippage can be inevitable (shouldn't be the majority).  Things change and shift at customers. 

 

We have "close lost" reasons/details, what if we have "Close date push details" ?

MA and I explain pushed opportunities weekly on the forecast call, which requires constant awareness and knowledge of the opportunities progress. What if we could have this in an easier way?

 

 

Remediation: Enforce "Why is it pushed"

What if we make it structured.  Not as a free-text (excuse) box.

If close date moves:

  • Required field: Reason for push
    • Budget not approved
    • Economic buyer not engaged
    • Legal delay
    • Competing priority
    • No confirmed timeline
    • Rep error (which forces accountability)

We can then review patterns monthly.

And then we can orient the coaching around the pattern.

 

Granted, this is limited details.  Maybe there is a field for details...

 

 

I don't want to make this more complicated, but I think we need to bring more evidence and make the reps more accountable.

 

 


Frederic Gingras

Sr Director, Account Management

514-913-9153

aptum.com | LinkedIn
